metadata.summary: Html Content / Article Extractor, web scrapping for Python3

documentation:
# Goose3 - Article Extractor

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache_2.0-blue.svg
:alt: License
:target: https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0/
```

```{image} https://github.com/goose3/goose3/workflows/Python%20package/badge.svg?branch=master
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://github.com/goose3/goose3/actions?query=workflow%3A%22Python+package%22
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/github/release/goose3/goose3.svg
:alt: GitHub release
:target: https://github.com/goose3/goose3/releases
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/goose3/goose3/branch/master/graph/badge.svg?token=PoWLaCLbW1
:alt: Test Coverage
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/goose3/goose3
```

```{image} https://badge.fury.io/py/goose3.svg
:alt: PyPi Release
:target: https://badge.fury.io/py/goose3
```

```{image} http://pepy.tech/badge/goose3
:alt: Downloads
:target: https://pepy.tech/project/goose3
```

## Intro

Goose was originally an article extractor written in Java that has most
recently (Aug2011) been converted to a [scala project](https://github.com/GravityLabs/goose).

This is a complete rewrite in Python. The aim of the software is to
take any news article or article-type web page and not only extract what
is the main body of the article but also all meta data and most probable
image candidate.

Goose will try to extract the following information:

- Main text of an article
- Main image of article
- Any YouTube/Vimeo movies embedded in article
- Meta Description
- Meta tags

The Python version was originally rewritten by:

- Xavier Grangier

## Licensing

If you find Goose useful or have issues please drop me a line. I'd love
to hear how you're using it or what features should be improved.

Goose is licensed by Gravity.com under the Apache 2.0 license; see the
LICENSE file for more details.

## On-line Documentation

On-line documentation is available on
[Read the Docs](https://goose3.readthedocs.io) which contains more in-depth
documentation.

## Setup

To install using pip, with all supported languages, which will install additional dependencies:

```
pip install goose3[all]
```

To install the minimal version:

```
pip install goose3
```

To install just the dependencies for a single language:

```
pip install goose3[chinese]
pip install goose3[arabic]
```

To install from source:

```
mkvirtualenv --no-site-packages goose3
git clone https://github.com/goose3/goose3.git
cd goose3
pip install -r ./requirements/python
python setup.py install
```

## Take it for a spin

```python
>>> from goose3 import Goose
>>> url = 'http://edition.cnn.com/2012/02/22/world/europe/uk-occupy-london/index.html?hpt=ieu_c2'
>>> g = Goose()
>>> article = g.extract(url=url)
>>> article.title
u'Occupy London loses eviction fight'
>>> article.meta_description
"Occupy London protesters who have been camped outside the landmark St. Paul's Cathedral for the past four months lost their court bid to avoid eviction Wednesday in a decision made by London's Court of Appeal."
>>> article.cleaned_text[:150]
(CNN) - Occupy London protesters who have been camped outside the landmark St. Paul's Cathedral for the past four months lost their court bid to avoi
>>> article.top_image.src
http://i2.cdn.turner.com/cnn/dam/assets/111017024308-occupy-london-st-paul-s-cathedral-story-top.jpg
```

## Configuration

There are two ways to pass configuration to goose. The first one is to
pass goose a Configuration() object. The second one is to pass a
configuration dict.

For instance, if you want to change the userAgent used by Goose just
pass:

```python
>>> g = Goose({'browser_user_agent': 'Mozilla'})
```

Switching parsers: Goose can now be used with lxml html parser or lxml
soup parser. By default the html parser is used. If you want to use the
soup parser pass it in the configuration dict :

```python
>>> g = Goose({'browser_user_agent': 'Mozilla', 'parser_class':'soup'})
```

One can also set Goose to be more lenient on network exceptions. To turn off
throwing all network exceptions, set the strict configuration setting to false:

```python
>>> g = Goose({'strict': False})
```

To turn on image fetching, one can simply enable it using the enable_image_fetching
configuration property:

```python
>>> g = Goose({'enable_image_fetching': True})
```

## Goose is now language aware

For example, scraping a Spanish content page with correct meta language
tags:

```python
>>> from goose3 import Goose
>>> url = 'http://sociedad.elpais.com/sociedad/2012/10/27/actualidad/1351332873_157836.html'
>>> g = Goose()
>>> article = g.extract(url=url)
>>> article.title
u'Las listas de espera se agravan'
>>> article.cleaned_text[:150]
u'Los recortes pasan factura a los pacientes. De diciembre de 2010 a junio de 2012 las listas de espera para operarse aumentaron un 125%. Hay m\xe1s ciudad'
```

Some pages don't have correct meta language tags, you can force it using
configuration :

```python
>>> from goose3 import Goose
>>> url = 'http://www.elmundo.es/elmundo/2012/10/28/espana/1351388909.html'
>>> g = Goose({'use_meta_language': False, 'target_language':'es'})
>>> article = g.extract(url=url)
>>> article.cleaned_text[:150]
u'Importante golpe a la banda terrorista ETA en Francia. La Guardia Civil ha detenido en un hotel de Macon, a 70 kil\xf3metros de Lyon, a Izaskun Lesaka y '
```

Passing {'use_meta_language': False, 'target_language':'es'} will
forcibly select Spanish.

## Video extraction

```python
>>> import goose3
>>> url = 'http://www.liberation.fr/politiques/2013/08/12/journee-de-jeux-pour-ayrault-dans-les-jardins-de-matignon_924350'
>>> g = goose3.Goose({'target_language':'fr'})
>>> article = g.extract(url=url)
>>> article.movies
[<goose.videos.videos.Video object at 0x25f60d0>]
>>> article.movies[0].src
'http://sa.kewego.com/embed/vp/?language_code=fr&playerKey=1764a824c13c&configKey=dcc707ec373f&suffix=&sig=9bc77afb496s&autostart=false'
>>> article.movies[0].embed_code
'<iframe src="http://sa.kewego.com/embed/vp/?language_code=fr&playerKey=1764a824c13c&configKey=dcc707ec373f&suffix=&sig=9bc77afb496s&autostart=false" frameborder="0" scrolling="no" width="476" height="357"/>'
>>> article.movies[0].embed_type
'iframe'
>>> article.movies[0].width
'476'
>>> article.movies[0].height
'357'
```

## Goose in Chinese

Some users want to use Goose for Chinese content. Chinese word
segmentation is way more difficult to deal with than occidental
languages. Chinese needs a dedicated StopWord analyser that need to be
passed to the config object.

```python
>>> from goose3 import Goose
>>> from goose3.text import StopWordsChinese
>>> url  = 'http://www.bbc.co.uk/zhongwen/simp/chinese_news/2012/12/121210_hongkong_politics.shtml'
>>> g = Goose({'stopwords_class': StopWordsChinese})
>>> article = g.extract(url=url)
>>> print article.cleaned_text[:150]
香港行政长官梁振英在各方压力下就其大宅的违章建筑（僭建）问题到立法会接受质询，并向香港民众道歉。

梁振英在星期二（12月10日）的答问大会开始之际在其演说中道歉，但强调他在违章建筑问题上没有隐瞒的意图和动机。

一些亲北京阵营议员欢迎梁振英道歉，且认为应能获得香港民众接受，但这些议员也质问梁振英有
```

## Goose in Arabic

In order to use Goose in Arabic you have to use the StopWordsArabic
class.

```python
>>> from goose3 import Goose
>>> from goose3.text import StopWordsArabic
>>> url = 'http://arabic.cnn.com/2013/middle_east/8/3/syria.clashes/index.html'
>>> g = Goose({'stopwords_class': StopWordsArabic})
>>> article = g.extract(url=url)
>>> print article.cleaned_text[:150]
دمشق، سوريا (CNN) - أكدت جهات سورية معارضة أن فصائل مسلحة معارضة لنظام الرئيس بشار الأسد وعلى صلة بـ"الجيش الحر" تمكنت من السيطرة على مستودعات للأسل
```

## Goose in Korean

In order to use Goose in Korean you have to use the StopWordsKorean
class.

```python
>>> from goose3 import Goose
>>> from goose3.text import StopWordsKorean
>>> url='http://news.donga.com/3/all/20131023/58406128/1'
>>> g = Goose({'stopwords_class':StopWordsKorean})
>>> article = g.extract(url=url)
>>> print article.cleaned_text[:150]
경기도 용인에 자리 잡은 민간 시험인증 전문기업 ㈜디지털이엠씨(www.digitalemc.com).
14년째 세계 각국의 통신·안전·전파 규격 시험과 인증 한 우물만 파고 있는 이 회사 박채규 대표가 만나기로 한 주인공이다.
그는 전기전자·무선통신·자동차 전장품 분야에
```

## TODO

- Video html5 tag extraction

classes.name: goose3.configuration.ArticleContextPattern
documentation:
Help ensure correctly generated article context patterns

Args:
    attr (str): The attribute type: class, id, etc
    value (str): The value of the attribute
    tag (str): The type of tag, such as `article` that contains the main article body
    domain (str): The domain to which this pattern pertains (optional)
Note:
    Must provide, at a minimum, (attr and value) or (tag)

classes.name: goose3.configuration.PublishDatePattern
documentation:
Ensure correctly formed publish date patterns; to be used in conjuntion with the configuration
`known_publish_date_tags` property

Args:
    attr (str): The attribute type: class, id, etc
    value (str): The value of the attribute
    content (str): The name of another attribute (of the element) that contains the value
    subcontent (str): The name of a json object key (optional)
    tag (str): The type of tag, such as `time` that contains the publish date
    domain (str): The domain to which this pattern pertains (optional)
Note:
    Must provide, at a minimum, (attr and value) or (tag)

classes.name: goose3.configuration.AuthorPattern
documentation:
Ensures that the author patterns are correctly formed for use with the `known_author_patterns` of configuration

Args:
    attr (str): The attribute type: class, id, etc
    value (str): The value of the attribute
    content (str): The name of another attribute (of the element) that contains the value
    tag (str): The type of tag, such as `author` that contains the author information
    subpattern (str): A subpattern for elements within the main attribute

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.known_context_patterns
documentation:
list: The context patterns to search to find the likely article content

Note:
    Each entry must be a dictionary with the following keys: `attr` and `value` or just `tag`

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.known_context_patterns
documentation:
val must be an ArticleContextPattern, a dictionary, or list of dictionaries
e.g., {'attr': 'class', 'value': 'my-article-class'}
    or [{'attr': 'class', 'value': 'my-article-class'},
        {'attr': 'id', 'value': 'my-article-id'}]

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.known_publish_date_tags
documentation:
list: The tags to search to find the likely published date

Note:
    Each entry must be a dictionary with the following keys: `attribute`, `value`, and `content`.

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.known_publish_date_tags
documentation:
val must be a dictionary or list of dictionaries
e.g., {'attrribute': 'name', 'value': 'my-pubdate', 'content': 'datetime'}
    or [{'attrribute': 'name', 'value': 'my-pubdate', 'content': 'datetime'},
        {'attrribute': 'property', 'value': 'pub_time', 'content': 'content'}]

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.known_author_patterns
documentation:
list: The tags to search to find the likely published date

Note:
    Each entry must be a dictionary with the following keys: `attribute`, `value`, and `content`.

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.known_author_patterns
documentation:
val must be a dictionary or list of dictionaries
e.g., {'attrribute': 'name', 'value': 'my-pubdate', 'content': 'datetime'}
    or [{'attrribute': 'name', 'value': 'my-pubdate', 'content': 'datetime'},
        {'attrribute': 'property', 'value': 'pub_time', 'content': 'content'}]

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.strict
documentation:
bool: Enable `strict mode` and throw exceptions instead of swallowing them.

Note:
    Defaults to `True`

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.strict
documentation: set the strict property

Note:

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.http_timeout

str: The local path to store temporary files

Note:

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.browser_user_agent
documentation:
Browser user agent string to use when making URL requests

Note:
    Defaults to `Goose/{goose3.__version__}`

Examples:
    Using the non-standard browser agent string is advised when pulling frequently

    >>> config.browser_user_agent = 'Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10_7_2)'
    >>> config.browser_user_agent = 'AppleWebKit/534.52.7 (KHTML, like Gecko)'
    >>> config.browser_user_agent = 'Version/5.1.2 Safari/534.52.7'

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.browser_user_agent
documentation: set the browser user agent string

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.imagemagick_identify_path
documentation:
str: Path to the identify program that is part of imagemagick

Note:
    Defaults to `"/opt/local/bin/identify"`
Warning:
    Currently not used / implemented

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.imagemagick_identify_path
documentation: set the imagemagick identify program path

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.imagemagick_convert_path
documentation:
str: Path to the convert program that is part of imagemagick

Note:
    Defaults to `"/opt/local/bin/convert"`
Warning:
    Currently not used / implemented

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.imagemagick_convert_path
documentation: set the imagemagick convert program path

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.stopwords_class
documentation:
StopWords: The StopWords class to use when analyzing article content

Note:
    Defaults to the english stop words
Note:
    Current stop words available in `goose3.text` include: 

    `StopWords`, `StopWordsChinese`, `StopWordsArabic`, and `StopWordsKorean`

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.stopwords_class
documentation: set the stopwords class to use

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.target_language
documentation:
str: The default target language if the language is not extractable or if use_meta_language is set to False

Note:
    Default language is 'en'

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.target_language
documentation: set the target language property

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.use_meta_language
documentation:
bool: Determine if language should be extracted from the meta tags or not. If this is set to `False` then
the target_language will be used. Also, if extraction fails then the target_language will be utilized.

Note:
    Defaults to `True`

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.use_meta_language
documentation: set the use_meta_language property

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.enable_image_fetching
documentation:
bool: Turn on or off image extraction

Note:
    Defaults to `False`

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.enable_image_fetching
documentation: set the enable_image_fetching property

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.images_min_bytes
documentation:
int: Minimum number of bytes for an image to be evaluated to be the main image of the site

Note:
    Defaults to 4500 bytes

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.images_min_bytes
documentation: set the images_min_bytes property

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.pretty_lists
documentation:
bool: Specify if lists should be pretty printed in the cleaned_text output

Note:
    Defaults to `True`

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.pretty_lists
documentation: set if lists should be pretty printed

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.parse_lists
documentation: set if headers should be parsed

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.parse_headers
documentation:
bool: Specify if headers should be pulled or not in the cleaned_text output

Note:
    Defaults to `True`

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.parse_headers
documentation: set if headers should be parsed

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.keep_footnotes
documentation:
bool: Specify if footnotes should be kept or not in the cleaned_text output

Note:
    Defaults to `True`

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.keep_footnotes
documentation: set if headers should be parsed

methods.name: goose3.configuration.Configuration.get_parser
documentation:
Retrieve the current parser class to use for extraction

Returns:
    Parser: The parser to use

methods.name: goose3.extractors.content.ContentExtractor.get_language
documentation: Returns the language is by the article or the configuration language

methods.name: goose3.extractors.content.ContentExtractor.is_boostable
documentation:
a lot of times the first paragraph might be the caption under an image so we'll want to make sure if we're
going to boost a parent node that it should be connected to other paragraphs, at least for the first n
paragraphs so we'll want to make sure that the next sibling is a paragraph and has at least some substatial
weight to it

methods.name: goose3.extractors.content.ContentExtractor.get_siblings_content
documentation: adds any siblings that may have a decent score to this node

methods.name: goose3.extractors.content.ContentExtractor.get_siblings_score
documentation:
we could have long articles that have tons of paragraphs so if we tried to calculate the base score against
the total text score of those paragraphs it would be unfair. So we need to normalize the score based on the
average scoring of the paragraphs within the top node.
For example: if our total score of 10 paragraphs was 1000 but each had an average value of 100 then 100 should
be our base.

methods.name: goose3.extractors.content.ContentExtractor.update_score
documentation:
adds a score to the gravityScore Attribute we put on divs we'll get the current score then add the score
we're passing in to the current

methods.name: goose3.extractors.content.ContentExtractor.update_node_count
documentation: stores how many decent nodes are under a parent node

methods.name: goose3.extractors.content.ContentExtractor.is_highlink_density
documentation:
checks the density of links within a node, is there not much text and most of it contains linky shit?
if so it's no good

methods.name: goose3.extractors.content.ContentExtractor.get_score
documentation: returns the gravityScore as an integer from this node

methods.name: goose3.extractors.content.ContentExtractor.nodes_to_check
documentation: returns a list of nodes we want to search on like paragraphs and tables

methods.name: goose3.extractors.content.ContentExtractor.post_cleanup
documentation: remove any divs that looks like non-content, clusters of links, or paras with no gusto

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.check_large_images
documentation:
although slow the best way to determine the best image is to download them and check the actual dimensions
of the image when on disk so we'll go through a phased approach...
1. get a list of ALL images from the parent node
2. filter out any bad image names that we know of (gifs, ads, etc..)
3. do a head request on each file to make sure it meets our bare requirements
4. any images left over let's do a full GET request, download em to disk and check their dimensions
5. Score images based on different factors like height/width and possibly things like color density

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.fetch_images
documentation:
download the images to temp disk and set their dimensions
- we're going to score the images in the order in which they appear so images higher up will have more
  importance,
- we'll count the area of the 1st image as a score of 1 and then calculate how much larger or small
  each image after it is
- we'll also make sure to try and weed out banner type ad blocks that have big widths and small heights
  or vice versa
- so if the image is 3rd found in the dom it's sequence score would be 1 / 3 = .33 * diff in area from
  the first image

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.is_banner_dimensions
documentation:
returns true if we think this is kind of a bannery dimension like 600 / 100 = 6 may be a fishy
dimension for a good image

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.filter_bad_names
documentation: takes a list of image elements and filters out the ones with bad names

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.is_valid_filename
documentation: will check the image src against a list of bad image files we know of like buttons, etc...

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.get_images_bytesize_match
documentation: loop through all the images and find the ones that have the best bytez to even make them a candidate

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.check_link_tag
documentation: checks to see if we were able to find open link_src on this page

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.check_known_schemas
documentation:
checks to see if we were able to find the image via known schemas:

Supported Schemas
 - Open Graph
 - schema.org

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.get_local_image
documentation: returns the bytes of the image file on disk

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.check_known_elements
documentation:
in here we check for known image contains from sites we've checked out like yahoo,
techcrunch, etc... that have
* known  places to look for good images.
* TODO: enable this to use a series of settings files so people can define what the image ids/classes are on
        specific sites

methods.name: goose3.extractors.images.ImageExtractor.build_image_path
documentation:
This method will take an image path and build out the absolute path to that image
* using the initial url we crawled so we can find a link to the image if they use relative urls like
../myimage.jpg

documentation: Define what to do when the context manager exits

methods.name: goose3.Goose.close
documentation:
Close the network connection and perform any other required cleanup

Note:
    Auto closed when using goose as a context manager or when garbage collected

methods.name: goose3.Goose.extract
documentation:
Extract the most likely article content from the html page

Args:
    url (str): URL to pull and parse
    raw_html (str): String representation of the HTML page
Returns:
    Article: Representation of the article contents including other parsed and extracted metadata

documentation:
Close the network connection

Note:
    Auto closed when using goose as a context manager or when garbage collected

methods.name: goose3.Goose.__crawl
documentation: wrap the crawling functionality

Extract most likely article content and aditional metadata from a URL or previously fetched HTML document

Args:
    config (Configuration, dict): A configuration file or dictionary representation of the configuration file
Returns:
    Goose: An instance of the goose extraction object

modules.name: goose3.utils.encoding
documentation: Handle Encoding

functions.name: goose3.utils.encoding.smart_unicode
documentation:
Returns a unicode object representing 's'. Treats bytestrings using the 'encoding' codec.

If strings_only is True, don't convert (some) non-string-like objects.

functions.name: goose3.utils.encoding.is_protected_type
documentation:
Determine if the object instance is of a protected type.

Objects of protected types are preserved as-is when passed to force_unicode(strings_only=True).

functions.name: goose3.utils.encoding.force_unicode
documentation:
Similar to smart_unicode, except that lazy instances are resolved to strings, rather than kept as lazy objects.

If strings_only is True, don't convert (some) non-string-like objects.

functions.name: goose3.utils.encoding.smart_str
documentation:
Returns a bytestring version of 's', encoded as specified in 'encoding'.

If strings_only is True, don't convert (some) non-string-like objects.

functions.name: goose3.utils.fnv_1a
documentation:
Pure python implementation of the 64 bit fnv-1a hash
Args:
    key (str): The element to be hashed
    seed (int): Add a seed to the initial starting point (0 means no seed)
Returns:
    str: 64-bit hashed representation of key
Note:
    Uses the lower 64 bits when overflows occur

functions.name: goose3.utils.deprecated
documentation:
A simplistic decorator to mark functions as deprecated. The function
will pass a message to the user on the first use of the function

Args:
    message (str): The message to display if the function is deprecated
